commands.repair_reason: - [P2] There is a narrow mechanical repair: make the trusted-proxy proof reject mixed broad proxy source lists and add one focused regression test.; Needs attention: The diff still has a concrete security-diagnostic gap for mixed trusted proxy source lists.; Review finding: [P1] Reject mixed broad trusted-proxy entries before downgrading — src/commands/doctor-security.ts:144 (sha=7a8a6e0dc1578c4af7d6f7b6bfa179e377d76041)
commands.review_summary:
The branch updates doctor security diagnostics so complete trusted-proxy gateway auth is reported as an exposed-auth warning instead of unauthenticated critical, with regression coverage for the happy path and several invalid trusted-proxy cases.

PR surface: Source +110, Tests +127. Total +237 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main's doctor exposure check only treats token/password shared secrets as auth proof, while runtime and docs define trusted-proxy as a gateway auth mode. I did not run the CLI because this review was read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is a narrow mechanical repair: make the trusted-proxy proof reject mixed broad proxy source lists and add one focused regression test.
